refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity output of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage hauled), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare reported productivity against productivity_target. Cross-check against worksite safety plan compliance logs.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) without preventing task completion.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, payload, cycle status) to enable productivity measurement and operator situational awareness.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate autonomous vehicle with remote teleoperation fallback; safety services enforce hard constraints; telemetry aggregated in cloud dashboard for productivity KPI reporting.

DP-B: Operator-in-the-loop teleoperation (on-site or remote) with safety services as advisory overlays; local vehicle controller executes operator commands; telemetry streamed to edge gateway for real-time productivity calculation.

DP-C: Hybrid: autonomous task planner generates work sequences; operator supervises via teleoperation; safety services enforce geofencing and collision avoidance; telemetry fused from vehicle and external sensors for productivity audit.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Operator-in-the-loop teleoperation (on-site or remote) with safety services as advisory overlays; local vehicle controller executes operator commands; telemetry streamed to edge gateway for real-time productivity calculation.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency and fidelity directly couple to operator task execution speed (productivity).
Safety services constraints (speed limits, geofencing) may reduce cycle time if overly restrictive.
Telemetry accuracy couples to productivity measurement credibility.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is decoupled in the sense that operator command authority is primary (not subordinate to autonomous planner), and safety services are overlays (not hard blockers). This preserves operator flexibility for productivity while maintaining safety guardrails. Coupling is acceptable because it is intentional and traceable to FR requirements.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and worksite conditions are representative of customer's typical operations; productivity target is achievable with DP-B.
Risk: Teleoperation latency > 500 ms may degrade operator control precision, reducing productivity below target.
Risk: Safety services constraints (e.g., speed limit 5 km/h in geofence) may be incompatible with productivity target; requires negotiation with Safety Manager.
Risk: Telemetry data loss or clock skew may invalidate productivity measurement; requires robust logging and audit trail.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify with Safety Manager and Site Manager: (1) acceptable latency budget for teleoperation, (2) safety constraint thresholds that do not conflict with productivity target, (3) telemetry accuracy and audit requirements for KPI verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system boundary encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and their integration. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle position, payload, cycle status, fuel/battery, fault codes streamed to Site Manager dashboard and Tele-operator console for situational awareness and productivity KPI calculation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator (on-site or remote) issues task commands (dig, load, haul, dump) via teleoperation interface; commands routed to Vehicle controller.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services emits alerts (geofence breach, collision risk, speed limit violation) to Tele-operator and Safety Manager for situational awareness and intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication between Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and external systems (cloud dashboard, safety monitoring center). Supports Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and network-based coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is a system-level outcome that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment is high because all sub-components and interfaces are owned within NGHE Solution. Diffusion is minimal (5%) due to external dependencies on worksite infrastructure (network, power, safety plan) and customer operations.

Vehicle executes earthmoving tasks and generates telemetry, but does not own teleoperation interface, safety services logic, or productivity measurement. Productivity outcome depends on Vehicle performance (cycle time, payload) but also on operator skill (teleoperation) and safety constraints. Containment is moderate; diffusion is high because productivity is a system-level KPI, not a Vehicle-only responsibility.

Teleoperation enables operator command and monitoring, which directly influences task execution speed and productivity. However, Teleoperation does not own Vehicle performance or safety services constraints. Containment is good for operator interface and command routing; diffusion is moderate because productivity also depends on Vehicle capability and safety guardrails.

Safety Services enforces constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) that impact productivity. However, Safety Services does not own task execution or teleoperation. Containment is moderate; diffusion is high because safety constraints are cross-cutting and affect all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, task planning).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is a system-level outcome that cannot be fully contained within any single subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The NGHE Solution boundary is the appropriate owner because it integrates all subsystems and is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery while maintaining safety compliance. Verification requires system-level testing (representative operations trial) and cross-subsystem data integration (telemetry aggregation, safety constraint audit).
containment_risks: Risk: Productivity measurement depends on accurate telemetry from Vehicle and external sensors; data quality issues (latency, loss, clock skew) may invalidate KPI verification.
Risk: Safety constraints imposed by Safety Services may conflict with productivity target; requires explicit negotiation and trade-off analysis with Safety Manager.

Risk: Operator skill and worksite conditions are external variables that influence productivity; NGHE Solution cannot fully control these factors. Verification must account for representative operations and baseline productivity assumptions.
Risk: Teleoperation latency and fidelity directly impact operator task execution speed; if Network Link is unreliable or congested, productivity may degrade below target.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit SLAs for Network Link (latency, availability, bandwidth) and telemetry accuracy (update rate, data loss tolerance) to ensure productivity target is achievable. Define safety constraint thresholds (speed limits, geofence sizes) in collaboration with Safety Manager to avoid conflicts with productivity target. Document baseline productivity assumptions (operator skill level, worksite conditions, task mix) for verification.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (operator-in-the-loop teleoperation with safety overlays) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 by enabling operator task execution with real-time telemetry for productivity measurement. The DP is decoupled in the sense that operator command authority is primary, and safety services are advisory overlays, preserving operator flexibility. Coupling is intentional and traceable. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable with clarification from Safety Manager and Site Manager.

diffusion_risks: Productivity KPI is a system-level outcome that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. No single subsystem can be held solely responsible for productivity; NGHE Solution boundary is appropriate owner.
Telemetry interface (out) is critical for productivity measurement; data quality and timeliness depend on Vehicle telemetry generation and Network Link reliability.
SafetyAlert interface (out) is critical for safety compliance; Safety Services must emit alerts in real-time to prevent safety violations that could impact productivity.
Tasking interface (in) is critical for operator command routing; latency and fidelity depend on Teleoperation subsystem and Network Link.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the integration of all subsystems and is responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery. Containment score is 95% because all sub-components and interfaces are within NGHE Solution scope. Diffusion is minimal (5%) due to external dependencies on worksite infrastructure and customer operations, which are outside system control but must be accounted for in verification.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial: log task completion (volume, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents over agreed period. Compare reported productivity against productivity_target. Cross-check against worksite safety plan compliance logs. Requires instrumentation of Vehicle (telemetry), Teleoperation (command logging), and Safety Services (constraint audit) to collect evidence.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM boundary. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Allocation is clear and traceable. Verification strategy is defined at system level (productivity KPI trial) and subsystem level (cycle time, latency, constraint compliance).

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit SLAs for Network Link (latency ≤ 500 ms, availability ≥ 99.5%, bandwidth ≥ 10 Mbps) to ensure teleoperation fidelity and telemetry timeliness.
Define safety constraint thresholds (speed limits, geofence sizes, collision avoidance margins) in collaboration with Safety Manager to ensure compatibility with productivity target.
Document baseline productivity assumptions (operator skill level, worksite conditions, task mix, representative operations definition) for verification and acceptance criteria.
Establish telemetry accuracy requirements (update rate ≥ 10 Hz, data loss tolerance ≤ 1%, clock skew ≤ 100 ms) to ensure productivity measurement credibility.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that requires decomposition into subsystem-level functional requirements (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) to enable detailed design and verification. The refined FR and sub-FRs are well-defined and atomic at the subsystem level. DP-B is selected and assigned to NGHE Solution IM boundary with clear external interfaces and containment analysis. Gate review passes with recommended clarifications on SLAs, safety constraints, and baseline assumptions. Next step is to decompose each sub-FR (Vehicle task execution, Teleoperation command/monitoring, Safety Services constraints, Telemetry measurement) to the next level of detail.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) without preventing task completion.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide real-time telemetry (position, payload, cycle status) to enable productivity measurement and operator situational awareness.

assumptions: Operator skill and worksite conditions are representative of customer's typical operations.
Productivity target is achievable with operator-in-the-loop teleoperation and safety overlays.
Network Link provides latency ≤ 500 ms and availability ≥ 99.5% for teleoperation and telemetry.
Safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing) are compatible with productivity target; conflicts will be resolved via negotiation with Safety Manager.
Telemetry data quality (update rate, loss tolerance, clock skew) is sufficient for productivity KPI measurement and audit.
Representative operations trial is feasible and representative of customer's actual usage patterns.
open_risks: Teleoperation latency > 500 ms may degrade operator control precision, reducing productivity below target.
Safety services constraints may be incompatible with productivity target; requires explicit negotiation and trade-off analysis.
Telemetry data loss or clock skew may invalidate productivity measurement; requires robust logging and audit trail.
Operator skill variability and worksite condition variability may introduce uncertainty in productivity measurement; baseline assumptions must be documented.
Network Link congestion or outages may disrupt teleoperation and telemetry, impacting productivity and safety.
evidence_needed: SLA specifications for Network Link (latency, availability, bandwidth) from Teleoperation subsystem design.
Safety constraint thresholds (speed limits, geofence sizes, collision avoidance margins) from Safety Services design and Safety Manager approval.
Baseline productivity assumptions (operator skill level, worksite conditions, task mix) from Site Manager and customer operations data.
Telemetry accuracy specifications (update rate, data loss tolerance, clock skew) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystem designs.
Representative operations trial plan (duration, task mix, measurement methodology, acceptance criteria) from Project Manager and Site Manager.
Verification and validation strategy for productivity KPI (data collection, analysis, audit trail) from System Engineer and Quality/Maintenance team.
